paper_title: PREDICTION IN OFF-DESIGN OPERATION FOR THE HELICAL HEAT RECOVERY EXCHANGER

entities.id: Off-design operation of helical heat recovery exchanger
entities.description: Prediction of performance in conditions other than the original design specifications for helical heat exchangers.
The process of thermal energy exchange within a single phase of matter.
The reduction in pressure as a fluid flows through a heat exchanger.
A mathematical model developed to predict heat transfer and pressure loss without requiring internal geometrical information.
Log Mean Temperature Difference method used to determine the temperature driving force for heat transfer.
Effectiveness-Number of Transfer Units method used to calculate the rate of heat transfer in heat exchangers.
Empirical correlation for computing heat transfer coefficients inside helical tubes.
Correlation used for shell-side heat transfer coefficients in tube bundles.
Engineering Equation Solver software used to program and solve the mathematical models.
The setup including heaters, pumps, and sensors used to validate the mathematical model.
Data collected from the experimental apparatus to validate simulation results.

entities.id: Differences between calculation and experiment (±1 to ±5%)
entities.description: The tolerance range showing agreement between the model and experimental results.
The measurement error margin for pressure difference sensors.
The temperature measurement resolution of the sensors used.
The minimum detectable change in water volumetric flow rate.
The primary research field of the journal issue.
